title: Grapple Pit
desc: The close-combat pit needs to be raked back into shape before the next round of formation drills begins.

steps.text:
The grapple pit is where the Barracks stop pretending that training can remain neat. Sand ridges mark lane edges, impact grooves show where heavier bodies hit harder than expected, and the central ring still carries the shallow trench left by this morning's squad exercise. Nothing in the pit is damaged exactly. It is simply too honest about what happened there.

The drill master plants the butt of a rake in the sand and surveys the mess with approval so dry it almost counts as praise. "Rake the lane ridges back into line if you want the broad work," she says. "Or reset the padded strike posts around the ring if you prefer the heavier lifting. The next squad will learn better if the ground stops teaching the previous lesson."

options.label: Rake the ridges and smooth the lane edges
Reset the padded strike posts around the ring

steps.text:
The lane ridges matter because feet trust them before the mind does. Once they flatten, trainees drift, spacing collapses, and a formation drill turns into an argument conducted with momentum. You draw the rake through the sand in long measured pulls, rebuilding the low walls that separate lane from lane and filling the heel cuts left by bodies that hit the turns too aggressively. By the time you finish, the pit no longer looks chewed. It looks deliberate.

The drill master walks one lane, watching how the sand takes her weight and how the ridge holds its line. That is all the inspection the work needs. "Good," she says. "Now the next squad can fail for its own reasons."

options.label: Lean the rake against the wall and leave the pit ready

steps.text:
The strike posts are thick cores wrapped in layered pads and sunk deep enough to survive creatures who have every intention of proving something to them. Two have shifted from center and another has sunk crooked where the retaining sand gave way. You brace each post, drag it back into its footing, and repack the base until the whole ring stands straight again, evenly spaced and equally available for violence in the name of discipline.

When the last post stops wobbling, the drill master tests it with a sharp shoulder-check that would have folded lesser equipment. The post holds. She seems satisfied by that in the way only a military trainer can be. "Better. If something is meant to absorb impacts all day, it should at least begin the day optimistic."

options.label: Brush the sand from your hands and reopen the ring
